data.name: ACDC-info
data.value: Thunder from Australia, the living embodiment of the phrase "high voltage." AC/DC is a band that hasn't changed its style for decades because their rock 'n' roll is perfect. Since 1973, the Young brothers have been creating music that makes you can't stand still. The grimy sound of Angus Young's Gibson SG and his unchanging school uniform have become the hallmarks of stadium-level excitement. Their rhythm section works like a Swiss watch, and their vocals pierce any wall. Through vocalist changes and tragedy, they have remained true to a simple, powerful sound that makes stadiums shake. If you're ready for a real shock, this video is your one-way ticket to the "highway to hell." Fasten your seatbelts: a current of several thousand volts is about to hit your veins!

data.name: BMTH_info
data.value: The leading chameleons of the modern heavy metal scene, having evolved from harsh deathcore to a futuristic rock sound. Oliver Sykes and his band from Sheffield have never been afraid to experiment, blending metal with electronics, pop, and orchestras. They have become a symbol of evolution, constantly challenging expectations and creating trends that the world follows. Their show is a high-tech immersion into a world of visual art and powerful sound, where old hits sit alongside cutting-edge compositions. The band transcends genres, creating something completely new and exciting. The time for experimentation is over; the time for pure action has arrived.

data.name: BonJovi_info
data.value: Stadium rock heroes from New Jersey, who conquered the world with their sincerity and flawless melodies. Jon Bon Jovi and his band proved that rock can be not only heavy, but also incredibly soulful. In the '80s, they became a symbol of the era, combining the drive of glam rock with lyrics about the lives of ordinary guys and big dreams. Their ballads are sung by the whole world, and their action songs make entire cities jump. This is music about hope, love, and about never giving up, even if you're "living on a prayer." On stage, they become a single machine, giving fans a sense of celebration and freedom. Each of their shows is a gigantic choir of thousands of voices. Feel the atmosphere of unity and drive that reigns at their performances.

data.name: Easter_egg
data.value: I'll sell my soul for tickets to an AC/DC concert.

data.name: KISS_info
data.value: Gods of thunder and rock 'n' roll, bursting onto the stage in a cloud of fire and makeup. KISS isn't just a band, it's the greatest show on the planet, which began in New York City in 1973. Four guys—Demon, Starchild, E.T., and Cat—created an entire universe of heavy riffs, high platforms, and exploding pyrotechnics. Over the decades, they've sold over 100 million albums, but their true element has always been in stadiums. Each of their performances is a ritual, where blood, fire, and guitars merge into a frenzied drive. Their anthems are known by heart by generations, and the KISS Army is considered the most devoted fan base in the world. At their concerts, you can see legends transform the night into a blazing flame.

data.name: Metallica_info
data.value: Absolute titans who rewrote the rules of heavy music and turned thrash metal into a global phenomenon. Emerging from the garages of San Francisco in the early '80s, Hetfield and Ulrich created the foundation upon which the entire modern scene stands. Their journey is a story of overcoming, tragedy, and resounding victories, cementing their status as the planet's premier metal band. From furious, high-speed thrashers to profound ballads, their music penetrates the very essence of human rage and hope. Millions of fans, endless touring, and a sound recognizable from the very first note. The lights dim, the legendary theme song appears on screens, and the first chords pierce the silence.

data.name: Ozzy_info
data.value: The Prince of Darkness, the great and terrible—the man who became synonymous with the word "heavy metal." Ozzy's journey began in foggy Birmingham with Black Sabbath, where he laid the foundations for the darkest and heaviest sound in history. But his solo career proved that Ozzy's madness knows no bounds. Throughout his ups and downs, he remains the "iron man" of the rock scene, whose voice is unmistakable. His charisma is simultaneously frightening and inspiring, and his concerts are always a balance on the brink of chaos. Despite all the rumors and legends, Ozzy remains devoted to his fans and the music that chills to the bone. Watch this good old madman take over the stage, making thousands of hearts beat in unison. The darkness deepens, the light fades... meet the legend at the very center of his musical empire!

data.name: Rammstein_info
data.value: The roar of a German industrial machine, merciless and uncompromising. Since 1994, six guys from East Berlin have been transforming music into a scorching fusion of metal, electronics, and pure fire. Till Lindemann and his team have created an aesthetic where provocation meets perfect sonic discipline. Their performances are more than just music; they are a total theater of pyrotechnics, where every riff is backed by a column of flame, and every word sinks into the consciousness with its weight. Monumental sets, caustic black humor, and a rhythm that moves stadiums around the world. The energy accumulated over the years reaches a critical point, and the machines begin to move.

data.name: Skillet_info
data.value: Preachers of drive and powerful alternative rock, proving that bright energy can be truly heavy. Skillet is a unique fusion of hard guitar riffs, symphonic arrangements, and piercing male and female vocals. John Cooper and his band have created music that becomes an anthem for those seeking strength in the darkest times. Their songs are full of struggle, faith, and an unbreakable spirit, making every performance incredibly emotional and honest. Their incredible dedication on stage and connection with the audience transform their show into a powerful energy boost. The tension in the audience rises, the spotlights focus on one spot, and the opening bars of the legendary hit ring out.

data.name: Slipknot_info
data.value: Nine masks embodying the collective nightmare and primal chaos of Iowa. Slipknot burst onto the scene at the turn of the century, bringing with them an unprecedented level of aggression, percussive madness, and visual terror. They're not just a band, they're a cult, uniting "maggots" around the world under the banner of total self-expression and pain. Behind the terrifying imagery lies a masterful mastery of sound, where heaviness meets melody, and fury meets profound meaning. Each of their shows is a controlled explosion, where the line between stage and crowd is erased in a single burst of madness. The masks are on, the drums begin their merciless march, and the cage opens.

data.name: Survey
data.value: Which of these bands do you like the most?

data.name: TDG_info
data.value: Masters of alternative rock from Canada, whose songs have become the voice of an entire generation experiencing internal turmoil. Three Days Grace masterfully convey feelings of pain, dependence, and the struggle to be oneself. Their music is built on honest lyrics and punchy choruses that make stadiums sing in unison. The change of vocalist only confirmed that the band's spirit is stronger than any circumstance, and their commitment to a hard and melodic sound remains unwavering. Each performance is an outpouring of pent-up emotions, allowing everyone in the audience to feel they are not alone in their battle. The guitar produces a familiar overdriven sound, and the audience is immersed in an atmosphere of heartfelt rock drive.
